items.title: Cape Verde stands out as LGBTQ rights progress amid challenges across Africa
items.description: Cape Verde is being recognised as one of Africa's most LGBTQ-friendly countries, offering legal protections that remain rare across the continent. But despite growing acceptance, members of the community say the journey toward full equality is far from over.

items.title: Trump reports more than $1.4 billion in income from crypto ventures
items.description: In his annual disclosure for 2025, the U.S. President received $635 ‌million from sale of his $TRUMP ​meme coins

items.title: Peruvian political heir Fujimori wins presidency
items.description: The 51-year-old daughter of late President Alberto Fujimori secured the top office on her fourth attempt

items.title: Germany out of 2026 World Cup: What went wrong?
items.description: Germany are out of the World Cup earlier than expected for the third straight time. How did this happen?

items.title: Morocco fans gather in Monterrey ahead of Round of 32 match against the Netherlands
items.description: A festive atmosphere in Monterrey on Sunday as Morocco fans gathered at the Macroplaza ahead of their team’s Round 32 World Cup match against the Netherlands.

items.title: The legal systems under which citizenship is acquired | Explained
items.description: What are the two different principles which govern citizenship laws in various countries? What was the system in the U.S.? How are India’s citizenship laws different?

items.title: Buenos Aires City poverty rate rises after five quarters of decline
items.description: Over 651,000 porteños – more than one in five residents – were living in poverty in the first quarter of 2026, according to the IDECBA city statistics bureau. Leer más

items.title: At least 12 dead in devastating Accra floods
items.description: The death toll from devastating floods in Ghana's capital, Accra, has risen to at least 12, with emergency officials warning the number could still increase as several people remain missing.

items.title: Pakistani airstrikes kill 36 civilians in Afghanistan and wound 160, officials say
items.description: Pakistan says it targeted militant hideouts along the Pakistan-Afghanistan border in response to militant attacks across Pakistan. Afghanistan condemned the strikes as acts of aggression.

items.title: Cape Verde extend World Cup 'dream' to set up Argentina clash
items.description: World Cup debutants Cape Verde will face Argentina in the knockout rounds after African side secured shock qualification from group stage. Leer más

items.title: Sudan's RSF committed crimes against humanity in el-Fasher, Amnesty says
items.description: The Amnesty report adds to evidence of atrocities in el-Fasher, which the UN says bore the hallmarks of genocide.

items.title: Fact check: As Europe bakes, should you trust your sunscreen?
items.description: As a heat wave sweeps across Europe, claims are resurfacing online questioning whether sunscreen is safe or linked to skin cancer risk.

items.title: Middle East live: Israeli troops will stay ‘indefinitely’ in Lebanon, Syria and Gaza, Katz says
items.description: Israeli forces will remain in so-called security zones established in Lebanon, Syria and Gaza, without any timeline for withdrawal, Israeli Defence Minister Israel Katz said Wednesday. In Qatar, an Iranian team holds talks with mediators but Iranian officials denied a claim by President Donald Trump that there would be direct talks with the US team. Follow our liveblog for the latest updates.

items.title: More than one million migrants apply for legal status in Spain
items.description: More than one million undocumented migrants and asylum seekers have applied for legal residency under Spain's mass regularisation programme, marking a significant response to one of Europe's largest immigration initiatives.

items.title: EU tech chief and Apple CEO had a constructive talk on topics of common interest
Thousands march to demand illegal migrants leave South Africa
items.description: From Johannesburg to Durban, thousands marched across South Africa demanding undocumented migrants leave the country. The nationwide protests followed weeks of demonstrations by citizen-led groups that set an unofficial June 30 deadline for migrants without legal status to depart.

items.title: Global firms and German startups compete in realizing nuclear fusion
items.description: Companies around the world are competing to see who can build the first commercially viable nuclear fusion reactor. German startups are also in the race, supported by major corporations and private investors.

items.title: India news: Kotak to buy Deutsche Bank retail operations
items.description: Kotak Mahindra Bank will take over the German bank's retail, private and weath portfolios in India while Deutche Bank will focus on ultra-high net worth clients. Meanwhile, jet fuel prices have fallen. DW has more.

items.title: Firefighters struggle to contain deadly Greek wildfire
items.description: More than 100 firefighters are fighting a fire near Thessaloniki which has claimed at least two lives.

items.title: Myanmar denies ASEAN request for Suu Kyi meeting
items.description: Suu Kyi has vanished from the public eye and is serving a lengthy but unspecified sentence on a host of charges rights groups dismiss as fabricated

items.title: Corruption, our daily bread
items.description: An unrelenting cascade of corruption scandals enjoys political and judicial protection, while the government negotiates key appointments to put the brakes on cases at Comodoro Py. Leer más

items.title: Attackers shoot dead two Guards members in Iran: state media
items.description: It was not immediately clear who was behind the shooting but Tehran has frequently blamed Kurdish separatist groups in the area for previous violence

items.title: Trump made more than $1bn from crypto in first year back in office
items.description: The president's crypto income far outpaces his earnings from real estate and Trump-themed items such as watches.

items.title: Last-minute NDIS relief for designer featured on Gardening Australia
items.description: Garden designer Joshua Ruff has been given a six-month reprieve by the National Disability Insurance Agency (NDIA) after speaking out about changes to his NDIS plan.

items.title: Boeing says IT outage affected computer systems, applications
Watch: Young bald eagle takes first flight in California
items.description: A popular stream set up by the Friends of Big Bear Valley caught the moment a juvenile eagle nicknamed Luna spread its wings.

items.title: DRC opposition leader Sesanga banned from leaving the country
items.description: An opposition leader in the Democratic Republic of Congo said Monday he had been banned from leaving the country and stripped of his passport, condemning it as "intimidation" amid tensions with the government.

items.title: Online fraud and scams increase as vigilance falls
items.description: A rise in organised, industrial-scale offshore scam centres and cybercriminals using AI to make scams harder to detect have played a part in an increase in online scams.

items.title: World Cup 2026: Mbappé's brace sends France into round of 16 as Les Bleus demolish Sweden
Rescuers keep up search for survivors at collapsed La Guaira building
items.description: At least 1,700 people are known to have been killed in the disaster but hope remains that others can continue to be found alive.

items.title: At least 14 children killed in Pakistan’s Lahore when tutoring centre roof collapses
items.description: Preliminary reports showed the tutoring centre was unregistered and operating inside a privately owned ‌residential building under a dilapidated ​roof

items.title: Milei confirms Santilli as new Cabinet chief after Adorni exit
items.description: Government confirms Diego Santilli as new Cabinet chief; Former interior minister replaces Manuel Adorni, who resigned amid scandal. Leer más

items.title: Attempts to question FATF’s credibility often reflect fear of scrutiny: India at UN
items.description: India’s Permanent Representative to the UN Parvathaneni Harish said that countries facing adverse assessments should address the identified deficiencies, strengthen domestic enforcement, improve financial transparency, and demonstrate irreversible action against terror-financing networks

items.title: Hungary: Magyar's 'Operation Purgatory' tackles Orban system
items.description: The new Hungarian government is moving swiftly to dismantle the system of former Prime Minister Viktor Orban. The focus: fighting corruption, reforming the media and protecting democracy.

items.title: Birthright citizenship was for children of slaves, not for world to 'pile' into U.S.: Trump
items.description: On the very first day of his inauguration, Trump issued an executive order against birthright citizenship, which was struck down by a federal court in Seattle the next day

items.title: New Sydney park set to open to public after golf course carve up
items.description: The plan attracted controversy from the golfing community as the NSW government struggles to find more green spaces.

items.title: Warring parties in Mali committing serious rights abuses, HRW report finds
items.description: Warring parties in Mali are committing serious rights abuses, according to a new report from Human Rights Watch.

items.title: Poland orders Swedish submarines to bulk up navy
items.description: Poland has placed an order for three Swedish submarines in an effort to step up defense capabilities. The NATO member is modernizing its navy in the face of Russian aggression at its borders.

items.title: SCOTUS to rule on birthright citizenship. And, U.S. murder rate nears new low
items.description: The Supreme Court is expected to make a long-awaited ruling on birthright citizenship today, on the high court's last day of its term. And, the U.S. murder rate approaches a record low.

items.title: Manuel Adorni finally resigns as Cabinet chief after months of scandal
items.description: Cabinet chief finally steps down after months of pressure, citing relentless media attacks; Official admitted he concealed US$500,000 from his asset declarations. Resignation is a significant blow to Javier Milei's inner circle. Leer más

items.title: Angela Merkel unveils official portrait in Berlin
items.description: On Tuesday former German Chancellor Merkel presented the painting for the official portrait gallery of the country's heads of government. She had previously inspired many artists.

items.title: Nurse says emergency procedures plan at Maradona's home care site were 'not clear'
items.description: A nurse who treated Diego Maradona says emergency procedures during the football icon's home hospitalisation were poorly defined as the trial into his death continues. Leer más

items.title: Rights groups sue Ghana over US third-country deportations
items.description: Ghana is facing a legal challenge at West Africa's highest court over its role in accepting migrants deported from the United States under President Donald Trump's third-country deportation policy.

items.title: The end of the beginning
items.description: No less than two-thirds of Messi’s record-breaking 18 World Cup goals were scored since turning 35 – in other words, at the age of 'Papi Fútbol,' he is deadlier than in the prime of youth. Leer más

items.title: Rwanda does not have 'political will' to honour peace deal, DR Congo minister says
items.description: In an interview with FRANCE 24, DR Congo's government spokesperson and Minister of Communication Patrick Muyaya accused Kigali of not having the "political will to implement" the 2025 US-brokered peace deal between the two countries. Muyaya also called for stronger international pressure on Rwanda. "The United States should use more tools on Rwanda (...) It's not enough yet," he stated.
